title: Authentication Design
description: Deep prompts for login, password reset, sessions, tokens, MFA, recovery, and account lifecycle.
valueSummary: Model authentication states, dependency trust, recovery paths, token lifecycle, and production evidence as one system.

title: Authorization Design
description: RBAC, permission matrices, object ownership, IDOR/BOLA prevention, files, admin boundaries, and backend enforcement.
valueSummary: Turn product roles and ownership rules into explicit backend policy, deny rules, and safe cross-role tests.

title: SaaS / Multi-Tenant Security
description: Tenant isolation, memberships, scoped queries, search, exports, storage boundaries, caches, jobs, and logs.
valueSummary: Trace tenant context through every subsystem and define isolation invariants that can be verified before release.

title: Business Logic Security
description: Abuse cases for valuable workflows, credits, refunds, state transitions, race conditions, and repeated actions.
valueSummary: Make hidden business invariants explicit and produce controls for sequence, concurrency, retries, and reconciliation.

title: Payment / Checkout Security
description: Checkout integrity, payment webhooks, trusted prices, subscriptions, trials, entitlements, and reconciliation.
valueSummary: Review money and entitlement flows around authoritative state, idempotency, event ordering, and operational recovery.
expectedTemplates: Payment Retry and Duplicate Charge Review

title: API Security Architecture
description: Endpoint matrices, abuse limits, error handling, field-level updates, object access, and sensitive response data.
valueSummary: Produce an endpoint-by-endpoint contract for authentication, authorization, validation, exposure, limits, and evidence.

title: Admin & Internal Tools
description: Admin panels, impersonation, dangerous exports, support workflows, irreversible actions, and audit evidence.
valueSummary: Apply stronger identity, authorization, approval, observability, and recovery controls to privileged operations.

title: AI Feature Security
description: AI threat models, agent permissions, prompt injection defense, sensitive data, tools, and human approval.
valueSummary: Separate untrusted model context from deterministic policy while controlling data, tools, actions, and review gates.

title: Release Readiness
description: Full feature threat modeling, implementation review, developer questions, and evidence-based production decisions.
valueSummary: Convert security findings and open assumptions into owners, priority controls, release evidence, and an explicit verdict.
